page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d2s.5xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 88.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.4 GB of memory per core. A key hardware characteristic is the inclusion of 62,706 GB of local HDD storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is weak, falling in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench tests, with single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by the middle-50% scores in multi-threaded stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register in the average tier, though the overall Passmark memory score is weak.

Given the massive local HDD storage capacity and average multi-core performance, this instance is designed for storage-heavy workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute speeds. It is suitable for large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, and database operations, supported by average Redis SET and Passmark database benchmark results. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of local HDD storage relative to the compute and memory profile provides a specialized resource balance, though the older Intel Xeon architecture presents a trade-off in raw processing efficiency compared to newer compute-optimized instances.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d2s.5xlarge is a storage-oriented instance featuring 20 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with x86_64 architecture, 88.0 GB of RAM, and 62,706 GB of local HDD storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant trade-off: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression are weak, ranking in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, whereas multi-core CPU capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. The massive local storage capacity combined with dedicated multi-core resources makes this instance suitable for storage-intensive applications, distributed file systems, and large-scale data processing workloads where sequential storage capacity is prioritized over high-speed single-threaded processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d2s.5xlarge is a storage-focused instance with 20 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 88 GB RAM, and 62,706 GB of local HDD storage, offering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d2s.5xlarge features 20 dedicated Intel cores, 88 GB RAM, and 62,706 GB local HDD storage for storage-heavy workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense instance combining twenty dedicated cores with massive local HDD capacity for data-intensive and distributed storage workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
62,706 GB of bundled local HDD storage for high-capacity data needs
88.0 GB of system memory providing 4.4 GB per core
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Weak single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for distributed file systems, data warehousing, and large databases
